title: Charmed Life - Chapter 5 (Fragment 1)
text:
Chapter 2

2

C
 AT WAS ALARMED by Miss Larkins. She was the daughter of Mr. Larkins at the junk shop. She was young and pretty and fiercely red-headed. She wore the red hair piled into a bun on top of her head, from which red tendrils of hair escaped and tangled becomingly with earrings like hoops for parrots to sit on. She was a very talented clairvoyant and, until the story of the cat became known, Miss Larkins had been the pet of the neighborhood. Cat remembered that even his mother had given Miss Larkins presents.

Cat knew Miss Larkins was offering to tell his fortune out of jealousy of Gwendolen. “No. No, thank you very much,” he said, backing away from Miss Larkins’ little table spread with objects of divination. “It’s quite all right. I don’t want to know.”

But Miss Larkins advanced on him and seized him by his shoulders. Cat squirmed. Miss Larkins used a scent that shrieked VIOLETS! at him, her earrings swung like manacles, and her corsets creaked when she was close to. “Silly boy!” Miss Larkins said, in her rich, melodious voice. “I’m not going to hurt you. I just want to know. ”

“But—but I don’t,” Cat said, twisting this way and that.

“Hold still,” said Miss Larkins, and tried to stare deep into Cat’s eyes.

Cat shut his eyes hastily. He squirmed harder than ever. He might have got loose, had not Miss Larkins abruptly gone off into some kind of trance. Cat found himself being gripped with a strength that would have surprised him even in the Willing Warlock. He opened his eyes to find Miss Larkins staring blankly at him. Her body shook, creaking her corsets like old doors swinging in the wind. “Oh, please let go!” Cat said. But Miss Larkins did not appear to hear. Cat took hold of the fingers gripping his shoulders, and tried to prise them loose. He could not move them. After that, he could only stare helplessly at Miss Larkins’ blank face.

Miss Larkins opened her mouth, and quite a different voice came out. It was a man’s voice, brisk and kindly. “You’ve taken a weight off my mind, lad,” it said. It sounded pleased. “There’ll be a big change coming up for you now. But you’ve been awfully careless—four gone already, and only five left. You must take more care. You’re in danger from at least two directions, did you know?”

The voice stopped. By this time, Cat was so frightened that he dared not move. He could only wait until Miss Larkins came to herself, yawned, and let go of him in order to cover her mouth elegantly with one hand.

“There,” she said, in her usual voice. “That was it. What did I say?”

Finding Miss Larkins had no idea what she had said brought Cat out in goose pimples. All he wanted to do was to run away. He dashed for the door.

Miss Larkins pursued him, seized his arms again, and shook him. “Tell me! Tell me! What did I say?” With the violence of her shaking, her red hair came down in sheets. Her corsets sounded like bending planks. She was terrifying. “What voice did I use?” she demanded.

“A—a man’s voice,” Cat faltered. “Sort of nice, and no nonsense about it.”

vocabulary.definition: Feeling worried or frightened by something unexpected.
vocabulary.example: Cat was alarmed by Miss Larkins.

vocabulary.definition: Moving backwards slowly, especially in fear or reluctance.
vocabulary.example: He said, backing away from Miss Larkins’ little table.

vocabulary.definition: Without expression or understanding; vacant.
vocabulary.example: Miss Larkins was staring blankly at him.

vocabulary.definition: Quick, energetic, and lively.
vocabulary.example: It was a man’s voice, brisk and kindly.

vocabulary.definition: Not giving sufficient attention or thought to avoiding harm or errors.
vocabulary.example: You’ve been awfully careless—four gone already.

vocabulary.definition: A person who claims to have a supernatural ability to see events in the future or beyond normal perception.
vocabulary.example: She was a very talented clairvoyant.

vocabulary.definition: Close-fitting undergarments worn to shape the torso.
vocabulary.example: Her corsets creaked when she was close to.

vocabulary.definition: Making a high-pitched, harsh sound, often of wood or metal under strain.
vocabulary.example: Her corsets creaking like old doors swinging in the wind.

vocabulary.definition: Ran quickly or suddenly.
vocabulary.example: He dashed for the door.

vocabulary.definition: The practice of seeking to foresee future events or discover hidden knowledge by supernatural means.
vocabulary.example: The table spread with objects of divination.

vocabulary.definition: In a graceful and stylish manner.
vocabulary.example: To cover her mouth elegantly with one hand.

vocabulary.definition: Spoke hesitantly or with pauses, often from uncertainty or nervousness.
vocabulary.example: Cat faltered.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows strong feeling, intensity, or violence.
vocabulary.example: She was young and pretty and fiercely red-headed.

vocabulary.definition: Held tightly or firmly.
vocabulary.example: He found himself being gripped with a strength.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows no ability to do anything to help oneself.
vocabulary.example: He could only stare helplessly at Miss Larkins’ blank face.

vocabulary.definition: A feeling of envy or resentment toward someone else's success or possessions.
vocabulary.example: Miss Larkins was offering to tell his fortune out of jealousy of Gwendolen.

vocabulary.definition: Metal bands, chains, or shackles for fastening someone's hands or ankles.
vocabulary.example: Her earrings swung like manacles.

vocabulary.definition: Having a pleasant tune or melody; sweet-sounding.
vocabulary.example: In her rich, melodious voice.

vocabulary.definition: Arranged or heaped up in a pile or stack, here referring to hair arranged high on the head.
vocabulary.example: She wore the red hair piled into a bun on top of her head.

vocabulary.definition: Small raised spots on the skin; here figuratively means goosebumps.
vocabulary.example: Cat brought out in goose pimples.

vocabulary.definition: To force open or move something with effort.
vocabulary.example: Cat tried to prise the fingers loose.

vocabulary.definition: Followed someone with the intention of catching them.
vocabulary.example: Miss Larkins pursued him.

vocabulary.definition: A distinctive smell, especially one that is pleasant.
vocabulary.example: Miss Larkins used a scent that shrieked VIOLETS! at him.

vocabulary.definition: Took hold of something quickly and firmly.
vocabulary.example: Miss Larkins advanced on him and seized him by his shoulders.

vocabulary.definition: To wriggle or twist the body from discomfort or embarrassment.
vocabulary.example: Cat squirmed.

vocabulary.definition: Thin, curling parts, like strands of hair or plants.
vocabulary.example: From which red tendrils of hair escaped and tangled becomingly.

vocabulary.definition: A half-conscious state, often induced by hypnosis or meditation.
vocabulary.example: Miss Larkins abruptly went off into some kind of trance.

vocabulary.definition: Physical force intended to hurt or damage.
vocabulary.example: With the violence of her shaking.
